## 4. Per-Distortion Agent Analysis  (RAG + LangGraph + Pydantic)

> **Methodology:** Each distortion type runs through a 4-node agentic graph:
> `observe` (Turn 1, no labels) → `hypothesise` (Turn 2, RAG-augmented)
> → `verify` (stat tool calls) → `conclude` (final structured output).
> If verify finds the hypothesis inconsistent with data, the graph loops
> back to hypothesise with the tool results as corrective context.

### 4.1  Blur — 1496 failures (31.8%)

**Most misclassified true classes:** cat (287), dog (287), bird (250), horse (189), frog (182)  
**Most common wrong predictions:** cat (806), dog (690)  
**Avg distortion confidence:** 0.0000  
**Epoch distribution:** ep0:1293 · ep1:133 · ep2:70

**Images analysed (after semantic deduplication):**

| # | File | Role | True | Predicted | Dist. Conf. |
|---|------|------|------|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | <img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="11d18708dcb7ec467d65cc49a4dfb2df0902ecdc.png" width="64" title="11d18708dcb7ec467d65cc49a4dfb2df0902ecdc.png"/> | ⭕ typical · d=1.364 | cat | dog | `0.0000` |
| 2 | <img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="068ed314c91c9fc92f156fb8cf019553e24e5c5b.png" width="64" title="068ed314c91c9fc92f156fb8cf019553e24e5c5b.png"/> | ⭕ typical · d=1.495 | dog | cat | `0.0000` |
| 3 | <img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="20892075be67d073a8ea803dcea1facd9661f4c5.png" width="64" title="20892075be67d073a8ea803dcea1facd9661f4c5.png"/> | ⭕ typical · d=1.754 | frog | dog | `0.0000` |

#### 🗃️  RAG Context Retrieved

> ━━━ RETRIEVED SIMILAR FAILURES FROM PAST RUNS ━━━
> (top 5 most visually similar past failures — same distortion type, cosine similarity on pixel embeddings)
> 
>   Past Case 1  [run 1, sim=1.000]:  true=cat  →  predicted=dog  (dist_conf=0.0000)
>   Past Case 2  [run 3, sim=1.000]:  true=cat  →  predicted=dog  (dist_conf=0.0000)
>   Past Case 3  [run 4, sim=1.000]:  true=cat  →  predicted=dog  (dist_conf=0.0000)
>   Past Case 4  [run 2, sim=1.000]:  true=cat  →  predicted=dog  (dist_conf=0.0000)
>   Past Case 5  [run 1, sim=0.954]:  true=bird  →  predicted=cat  (dist_conf=0.0000)
> 
> Do these past cases share the same failure mechanism as the current images, or does the current batch represent a novel pattern?

#### Turn 1 — Visual Observations (no labels)

> The image shows a dog standing on its hind legs, with its front paws resting on the back of another dog. The dog in the foreground appears to be a small breed with a brown and white coat, possibly a Jack Russell Terrier or similar breed, and it is looking upwards towards the camera. The background is blurred but seems to be an indoor setting with a red surface that could be a carpet or a piece of furniture. There are no distinct artifacts visible in this image; however, the focus on the dog suggests that the surrounding area might have been intentionally blurred out for privacy or artistic effect. The surviving structure is the silhouette of the standing dog, which is clear and well-defined against the blurred background. The most degraded part of the image is the background due to the lack of focus on it. There is no common pattern across all three images as they are different instances of the same scene captured at different times or angles.

#### 🔧 Verification Tool Results

> • Blur accounts for 31.8% of all failures (1496 images). Most commonly misclassified: cat(287), dog(287), bird(250). Most common wrong predictions: cat(806), dog(690). Avg distortion classifier confidence: 0.0000.
> • 86% of blur failures in epoch 0 — model never learned to handle this distortion type. Strong signal: add this distortion to training augmentation.
> • Top confusion pairs for blur: cat→cat (806 cases), dog→dog (690 cases)
> • dog→dog appears 0 times (not in top 20 confusion pair, 0.0% of all failures).

#### Turn 2 — Synthesised Analysis (iteration 2)

**🔗 Shared Failure Pattern**

> Blur

**⭕↔⚠️  Typical vs Outlier**

> The image shows a dog standing on its hind legs, which is not a typical behavior for dogs. This could be due to the distortion type.

**❌ What Misled the Model**

> The blurred background and the silhouette of the dog might have caused the model to misclassify it as a cat, as it obscures some features that would normally help in identifying the animal. The distortion makes it difficult for the model to recognize the dog's typical posture.

**📊 Confidence Assessment**

> The distortion confidence values are low because the blurred background and silhouette of the dog make it challenging for the model to accurately classify the image.

**🎯 Root Cause**

> The root cause of this failure is the presence of a blurred background and the silhouette of the dog, which obscures the typical features that would help in identifying the animal. This distortion type on these images triggers the specific wrong-class prediction.

**🗃️  RAG — Novel vs Known Pattern**

> This batch represents a novel pattern not seen in retrieved historical cases.

#### Turn 1 — Visual Observations (no labels)

> The image shows a dog lying on the floor, and it appears to be affected by pixelation distortion. The pixelation gives the image a blocky, mosaic-like appearance with distinct squares of color. The dog is in the center of the image, and its body is mostly obscured by the pixelation, making it difficult to discern specific details about the dog's breed or condition. The background is indistinct due to the pixelation as well. There are no visible artifacts or regions that can be confidently identified within this image.

#### 🔧 Verification Tool Results

> • Pixelate accounts for 15.4% of all failures (724 images). Most commonly misclassified: airplane(167), deer(148), dog(102). Most common wrong predictions: horse(393), ship(331). Avg distortion classifier confidence: 0.0000.
> • 85% of pixelate failures in epoch 0 — model never learned to handle this distortion type. Strong signal: add this distortion to training augmentation.
> • Top confusion pairs for pixelate: airplane→horse (393 cases), deer→ship (331 cases)

#### Turn 2 — Synthesised Analysis (iteration 2)

**🔗 Shared Failure Pattern**

> Pixelation distortion

**⭕↔⚠️  Typical vs Outlier**

> The image shows a dog lying on the floor, which is typical of the dataset. The pixelation distortion affects all images in this batch and is not present in the retrieved historical cases.

**❌ What Misled the Model**

> The pixelation distortion makes it difficult to discern specific details about the dog's breed or condition, but it visually resembles a horse due to its blocky, mosaic-like appearance with distinct squares of color. This is likely what misled the model into predicting 'horse'.

**📊 Confidence Assessment**

> The distortion confidence values are at the shown level because the pixelation makes it difficult for the model to accurately classify the image, as it obscures details and context.

**🎯 Root Cause**

> The root cause of this failure is the pixelation distortion on these images, which triggers the specific wrong-class prediction of 'horse' by the neural network.

**🗃️  RAG — Novel vs Known Pattern**

> This batch represents a novel pattern not seen in retrieved historical cases.

#### Turn 1 — Visual Observations (no labels)

> The image shows a cat with a blurry background, which suggests that the photo may have been taken in low light or with a camera that has difficulty focusing. The cat appears to be indoors, as indicated by the artificial lighting and the lack of natural features like trees or outdoor elements. The cat's fur is striped, and it seems to be looking directly at the camera. There are no distinct artifacts visible in this image; however, the blurriness makes it difficult to discern specific details about the surroundings or any other objects that might be present.

#### 🔧 Verification Tool Results

> • Noise accounts for 29.3% of all failures (1381 images). Most commonly misclassified: deer(285), cat(247), bird(205). Most common wrong predictions: bird(626), deer(405), frog(290). Avg distortion classifier confidence: 0.0000.
> • 80% of noise failures in epoch 0 — model never learned to handle this distortion type. Strong signal: add this distortion to training augmentation.
> • Top confusion pairs for noise: deer→bird (626 cases), cat→deer (405 cases), bird→frog (290 cases)
> • cat→frog appears 86 times (rank #20 confusion pair, 1.8% of all failures).

#### Turn 2 — Synthesised Analysis (iteration 2)

**🔗 Shared Failure Pattern**

> Noise distortion

**⭕↔⚠️  Typical vs Outlier**

> The image shows a cat, which is typical of the noise distortion pattern. The outlier is an image of a frog.

**❌ What Misled the Model**

> The model was misled by the noise distortion in the image, which visually resembles a frog due to the pixelation and blurriness.

**📊 Confidence Assessment**

> The confidence level for the noise distortion is low (0.00000), indicating that it is subtle and not immediately obvious at 32x32 resolution.

**🎯 Root Cause**

> The root cause of this failure is the noise distortion, which confuses the model into classifying a cat as a frog due to its visual similarity to a frog under the given distortion.

**🗃️  RAG — Novel vs Known Pattern**

> This batch represents a novel pattern not seen in retrieved historical cases.

| **noise** | The root cause of this failure is the noise distortion, which confuses the model into classifying a cat as a frog due to its visual similarity to a frog under the given distortion. |
